content.full_text:
June 26, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Contributions by California General Purpose Recipient Committees to candidates for office in other states

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

After discussing the above issue with personnel at the Fair Political Practices Commission they suggested we contact you to seek your opinion on the above referenced issue.

One of our clients, a broad based general recipient committee located in California, has been solicited for monetary contributions by a candidate for the office of governor of the state of Alaska. Our client wishes to contribute up to $1,000 to this campaign, but has requested our assistance in determining the legality of such a contribution and the filing responsibilities, if any, that such a contribution would create.

Given the above, we request that you provide us with your opinion as to the legality of a contribution by our client of up to $1000 to a candidate for state office in another state. In addition, please provide us with a summary of any filing responsibilities as a result of such a contribution.

We appreciate your assistance in this matter. Please give us a call if you have any questions regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Nicholas J. Ross  
NJR:lsp
